english_name: The Second Epistle of Paul the Apostle to Timothy

verses.latin_vulgate: Testificor coram Deo, et Jesu Christo, qui judicaturus est vivos et mortuos, per adventum ipsius, et regnum ejus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διαμαρτύρομαι οὖν ἐγὼ ἐνώπιον τοῦ θεοῦ καὶ τοῦ κυρίου ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ τοῦ μέλλοντος κρίνειν ζῶντας καὶ νεκρούς κατὰ τὴν ἐπιφάνειαν αὐτοῦ καὶ τὴν βασιλείαν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: I charge *thee* therefore before God, and the Lord Jesus Christ, who shall judge the quick and the dead at his appearing and his kingdom;

verses.latin_vulgate: prædica verbum, insta opportune, importune: argue, obsecra, increpa in omni patientia, et doctrina.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κήρυξον τὸν λόγον ἐπίστηθι εὐκαίρως ἀκαίρως ἔλεγξον ἐπιτίμησον παρακάλεσον ἐν πάσῃ μακροθυμίᾳ καὶ διδαχῇ
verses.king_james: Preach the word; be instant in season, out of season; reprove, rebuke, exhort with all longsuffering and doctrine.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erit enim tempus, cum sanam doctrinam non sustinebunt, sed ad sua desideria coacervabunt sibi magistros, prurientes auribus,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔσται γὰρ καιρὸς ὅτε τῆς ὑγιαινούσης διδασκαλίας οὐκ ἀνέξονται ἀλλὰ κατὰ τὰς ἐπιθυμίας τὰς ἰδίας ἑαυτοῖς ἐπισωρεύσουσιν διδασκάλους κνηθόμενοι τὴν ἀκοήν
verses.king_james: For the time will come when they will not endure sound doctrine; but after their own lusts shall they heap to themselves teachers, having itching ears;

verses.latin_vulgate: et a veritate quidem auditum avertent, ad fabulas autem convertentur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀπὸ μὲν τῆς ἀληθείας τὴν ἀκοὴν ἀποστρέψουσιν ἐπὶ δὲ τοὺς μύθους ἐκτραπήσονται
verses.king_james: And they shall turn away *their* ears from the truth, and shall be turned unto fables.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu vero vigila, in omnibus labora, opus fac evangelistæ, ministerium tuum imple. Sobrius esto.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σὺ δὲ νῆφε ἐν πᾶσιν κακοπάθησον ἔργον ποίησον εὐαγγελιστοῦ τὴν διακονίαν σου πληροφόρησον
verses.king_james: But watch thou in all things, endure afflictions, do the work of an evangelist, make full proof of thy ministry.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego enim jam delibor, et tempus resolutionis meæ instat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ γὰρ ἤδη σπένδομαι καὶ ὁ καιρὸς τῆς ἐμῆς ἀναλύσεώς ἐφέστηκεν
verses.king_james: For I am now ready to be offered, and the time of my departure is at hand.

verses.latin_vulgate: Bonum certamen certavi, cursum consummavi, fidem servavi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸν ἀγῶνα τὸν καλὸν ἠγώνισμαι τὸν δρόμον τετέλεκα τὴν πίστιν τετήρηκα
verses.king_james: I have fought a good fight, I have finished *my* course, I have kept the faith:

verses.latin_vulgate: In reliquo reposita est mihi corona justitiæ, quam reddet mihi Dominus in illa die, justus judex: non solum autem mihi, sed et iis, qui diligunt adventum ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λοιπὸν ἀπόκειταί μοι ὁ τῆς δικαιοσύνης στέφανος ὃν ἀποδώσει μοι ὁ κύριος ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ἡμέρᾳ ὁ δίκαιος κριτής οὐ μόνον δὲ ἐμοὶ ἀλλὰ καὶ πάσιν τοῖς ἠγαπηκόσιν τὴν ἐπιφάνειαν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Henceforth there is laid up for me a crown of righteousness, which the Lord, the righteous judge, shall give me at that day: and not to me only, but unto all them also that love his appearing.

verses.latin_vulgate: Festina ad me venire cito.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σπούδασον ἐλθεῖν πρός με ταχέως
verses.king_james: Do thy diligence to come shortly unto me:

verses.latin_vulgate: Demas enim me reliquit, diligens hoc sæculum, et abiit Thessalonicam: Crescens in Galatiam, Titus in Dalmatiam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δημᾶς γάρ με ἐγκατέλιπεν ἀγαπήσας τὸν νῦν αἰῶνα καὶ ἐπορεύθη εἰς θεσσαλονίκην κρήσκης εἰς γαλατίαν τίτος εἰς δαλματίαν
verses.king_james: For Demas hath forsaken me, having loved this present world, and is departed unto Thessalonica; Crescens to Galatia, Titus unto Dalmatia.

verses.latin_vulgate: Lucas est mecum solus. Marcum assume, et adduc tecum: est enim mihi utilis in ministerium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λουκᾶς ἐστιν μόνος μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ μᾶρκον ἀναλαβὼν ἄγε μετὰ σεαυτοῦ ἔστιν γάρ μοι εὔχρηστος εἰς διακονίαν
verses.king_james: Only Luke is with me. Take Mark, and bring him with thee: for he is profitable to me for the ministry.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tychicum autem misi Ephesum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τυχικὸν δὲ ἀπέστειλα εἰς ἔφεσον
verses.king_james: And Tychicus have I sent to Ephesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Penulam, quam reliqui Troade apud Carpum, veniens affer tecum, et libros, maxime autem membranas.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τὸν φαιλόνην ὃν ἀπέλιπον ἐν τρῳάδι παρὰ κάρπῳ ἐρχόμενος φέρε καὶ τὰ βιβλία μάλιστα τὰς μεμβράνας
verses.king_james: The cloke that I left at Troas with Carpus, when thou comest, bring *with thee*, and the books, *but* especially the parchments.

verses.latin_vulgate: Alexander ærarius multa mala mihi ostendit: reddet illi Dominus secundum opera ejus:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλέξανδρος ὁ χαλκεὺς πολλά μοι κακὰ ἐνεδείξατο ἀποδῴη αὐτῷ ὁ κύριος κατὰ τὰ ἔργα αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Alexander the coppersmith did me much evil: the Lord reward him according to his works:

verses.latin_vulgate: quem et tu devita: valde enim restitit verbis nostris.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃν καὶ σὺ φυλάσσου λίαν γὰρ ἀνθέστηκεν τοῖς ἡμετέροις λόγοις
verses.king_james: Of whom be thou ware also; for he hath greatly withstood our words.

verses.latin_vulgate: In prima mea defensione nemo mihi affuit, sed omnes me dereliquerunt: non illis imputetur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν τῇ πρώτῃ μου ἀπολογίᾳ οὐδείς μοι συμπαρεγένετο ἀλλὰ πάντες με ἐγκατέλιπον μὴ αὐτοῖς λογισθείη
verses.king_james: At my first answer no man stood with me, but all *men* forsook me: *I pray God* that it may not be laid to their charge.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dominus autem mihi astitit, et confortavit me, ut per me prædicatio impleatur, et audiant omnes gentes: et liberatus sum de ore leonis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ κύριός μοι παρέστη καὶ ἐνεδυνάμωσέν με ἵνα δι᾽ ἐμοῦ τὸ κήρυγμα πληροφορηθῇ καὶ ἀκούσῃ πάντα τὰ ἔθνη καὶ ἐῤῥύσθην ἐκ στόματος λέοντος
verses.king_james: Notwithstanding the Lord stood with me, and strengthened me; that by me the preaching might be fully known, and *that* all the Gentiles might hear: and I was delivered out of the mouth of the lion.

verses.latin_vulgate: Liberavit me Dominus ab omni opere malo: et salvum faciet in regnum suum cæleste, cui gloria in sæcula sæculorum. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ῥύσεταί με ὁ κύριος ἀπὸ παντὸς ἔργου πονηροῦ καὶ σώσει εἰς τὴν βασιλείαν αὐτοῦ τὴν ἐπουράνιον ᾧ ἡ δόξα εἰς τοὺς αἰῶνας τῶν αἰώνων ἀμήν
verses.king_james: And the Lord shall deliver me from every evil work, and will preserve *me* unto his heavenly kingdom: to whom *be* glory for ever and ever. Amen.

verses.latin_vulgate: Saluta Priscam, et Aquilam, et Onesiphori domum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἄσπασαι πρίσκαν καὶ ἀκύλαν καὶ τὸν ὀνησιφόρου οἶκον
verses.king_james: Salute Prisca and Aquila, and the household of Onesiphorus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erastus remansit Corinthi. Trophimum autem reliqui infirmum Mileti.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔραστος ἔμεινεν ἐν κορίνθῳ τρόφιμον δὲ ἀπέλιπον ἐν μιλήτῳ ἀσθενοῦντα
verses.king_james: Erastus abode at Corinth: but Trophimus have I left at Miletum sick.

verses.latin_vulgate: Festina ante hiemem venire. Salutant te Eubulus, et Pudens, et Linus, et Claudia, et fratres omnes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σπούδασον πρὸ χειμῶνος ἐλθεῖν ἀσπάζεταί σε εὔβουλος καὶ πούδης καὶ λίνος καὶ κλαυδία καὶ οἱ ἀδελφοὶ πάντες
verses.king_james: Do thy diligence to come before winter. Eubulus greeteth thee, and Pudens, and Linus, and Claudia, and all the brethren.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dominus Jesus Christus cum spiritu tuo. Gratia vobiscum. Amen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ κύριος ἰησοῦς χριστὸς μετὰ τοῦ πνεύματός σου ἡ χάρις μεθ᾽ ὑμῶν ἀμήν
verses.king_james: The Lord Jesus Christ *be* with thy spirit. Grace *be* with you. Amen.
